touches.ai_summary: Cold call with Jude Murphy to introduce our platform. Jude expressed interest in onboarding automation capabilities after mentioning current manual processes are creating bottlenecks in CS. Brief intro call, agreed to send follow-up materials.

questions_asked.question: How is your team currently handling customer onboarding?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual - lots of back and forth emails, some spreadsheet tracking. It's becoming a real time sink as we scale.

next_steps: Send overview deck and case studies
Schedule qualification call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with product overview, PropTech case studies, and ROI calculator. Jude responded confirming availability for qualification call.
next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for Thursday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Jude to understand current state and pain points. DeltaVision is scaling rapidly (50% YoY growth) and onboarding is becoming a major bottleneck. Currently evaluating solutions including Gong. Team of ~30 CS reps, looking to automate repetitive onboarding tasks. Budget authority sits with CRO Sofia Hill. Jude is championing this internally.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We're onboarding 15-20 new customers per month now, up from 5-6 last year. The manual process doesn't scale and we're missing SLA targets.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in this decision?
questions_asked.answer: Sofia Hill, our CRO, has final say. IT will need to weigh in on integration. RevOps will probably want to kick the tires too.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform integrate with Salesforce and our existing tech stack?
questions_asked.answer: We have native Salesforce integration plus API access for custom workflows. Can connect to most common tools in your stack.

next_steps: Send technical integration docs
Schedule discovery call to map specific workflows
Intro to RevOps team

touches.ai_summary: Sent technical integration documentation and workflow mapping questionnaire. Jude confirmed she's sharing with RevOps and IT ahead of discovery call.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Jude to map current onboarding workflow in detail. Walked through their 6-stage onboarding process, identified 12+ automation opportunities. Discussed unlimited automation tier to handle their volume. Jude mentioned they're also looking at Gong but likes our focus on CS use cases vs sales-first tools.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your ideal onboarding flow if manual work wasn't a constraint.
questions_asked.answer: We'd have automated kickoff sequences, self-service resource portals, proactive check-ins triggered by usage data, and seamless handoffs between onboarding and account management.

questions_asked.question: What kind of analytics and reporting do you offer on onboarding performance?
questions_asked.answer: Full dashboard with time-to-value metrics, completion rates, bottleneck identification, and team performance analytics.

questions_asked.question: How do you compare to Gong for CS use cases?
questions_asked.answer: Gong is excellent for sales conversation intelligence, but we're purpose-built for CS workflows and automation. Different core focus.

next_steps: Set up technical evaluation with Jessica from RevOps
Prepare custom demo environment
Schedule follow-up discovery with broader team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with discovery notes, ROI projection based on their workflow, and intro to Jessica Martin for technical evaluation. Jude confirmed Jessica and Rowan from IT are looped in.
next_steps: Technical eval scheduled with Jessica for Monday

touches.ai_summary: Deep technical evaluation with Jessica Martin. Reviewed integration architecture, security requirements, API capabilities, and data handling. Jessica asked detailed questions about Salesforce sync, webhook configurations, and user provisioning. Walked through sandbox environment setup and testing protocols.

questions_asked.question: How does data residency work? We have some European customers with GDPR requirements.
questions_asked.answer: We offer EU data residency options and are fully GDPR compliant. Can configure per-customer data location rules.

questions_asked.question: What's your SLA on API uptime and support response times?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime SLA, 4-hour response for critical issues on enterprise plans, 24-hour for standard. Dedicated support channel included.

questions_asked.question: What are your key technical evaluation criteria?
questions_asked.answer: Integration complexity, security posture, scalability, and ease of maintenance. We don't want something that becomes a burden for our small IT team.

objections: Need to verify SSO compatibility with our Okta setup
next_steps: Provide SSO configuration guide
Set up sandbox access for testing
Schedule follow-up technical session

touches.ai_summary: Second discovery call with Jude, now joined by Rowan from IT. Discussed implementation timeline, training requirements, and change management. Rowan raised questions about ongoing maintenance and admin burden. Jude is pushing for quick implementation to hit Q2 onboarding targets.

questions_asked.question: How long does typical implementation take for a company our size?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 4-6 weeks from kickoff to production, including integrations, workflow configuration, and team training.

questions_asked.question: What kind of ongoing admin time should we expect?
questions_asked.answer: After initial setup, most customers spend 2-4 hours per week on workflow adjustments and monitoring. It's designed to be low-touch.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally finalize by end of month so we can start implementation in early May. Need to hit Q2 efficiency targets.

next_steps: Schedule demo for broader CS team
Provide implementation timeline and plan
Connect with Jessica for next technical session

touches.ai_summary: Sent detailed implementation plan, timeline, and training resources. Jude confirmed she's building internal business case for Sofia and needs pricing guidance for 28 seats.

questions_asked.question: Can you send over ballpark pricing for 28 seats on the unlimited automation tier?
questions_asked.answer: Will prepare a formal proposal, but typically $25-30K ACV range for your seat count and tier. Will include exact pricing in proposal.

next_steps: Continue technical evaluation with Jessica

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up technical evaluation with Jessica. Tested Salesforce integration in sandbox, reviewed webhook configurations, and validated SSO setup with Okta. Jessica confirmed technical requirements are met but wants to review security documentation with IT before final sign-off.

questions_asked.question: Do you have SOC 2 Type II certification?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, current SOC 2 Type II report available. Can provide under NDA.

questions_asked.question: Any technical blockers from your perspective?
questions_asked.answer: Nothing major. Need to finalize security review with Rowan, but integration testing went smoothly.

next_steps: Provide SOC 2 report and security documentation
Schedule final technical check with Rowan

touches.ai_summary: Sent SOC 2 Type II report and comprehensive security documentation package. Jessica confirmed receipt and is reviewing with IT team.
next_steps: Final technical evaluation session scheduled
Prepare formal proposal for Jude

touches.ai_summary: Final technical evaluation with Jessica and Rowan. Walked through security documentation, penetration testing results, and compliance certifications. Rowan signed off on technical requirements. Jessica mentioned they're also evaluating Gong but our CS-specific features are more aligned to their needs. Asked about volume discounting given their growth trajectory.

questions_asked.question: What does pricing look like if we grow to 40-50 seats over the next year?
questions_asked.answer: We offer volume tiers and can structure with growth pricing. Happy to include expansion pricing in the proposal.

questions_asked.question: Are we clear from a technical perspective to move forward?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, technical requirements are met. Jude can take this to Sofia for budget approval now.

next_steps: Prepare proposal with growth pricing options
Jude to present business case to Sofia

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Jude. Presented $29,800 ACV for 28 seats, unlimited automation tier. Jude mentioned this is above her initial budget expectation and asked about discount options. Discussed ROI based on time savings and mentioned Gong came in lower but lacks CS-specific features. Jude committed to taking this to Sofia but flagged pricing as potential concern.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing? We were hoping to land closer to $20K.
questions_asked.answer: Let me talk to my manager about what we can do. Given your growth potential and timeline, there may be room to work with you on first-year pricing.

questions_asked.question: What's Gong's pricing looking like for comparison?
questions_asked.answer: They came in around $22K, but it's really a sales tool. I'm advocating for you because the CS fit is much better, but I need to make the numbers work for Sofia.

objections: Pricing is above initial budget expectations
Competing offer from Gong is lower
next_steps: Explore discount options with leadership
Schedule exec meeting with Sofia
Provide ROI calculator for Jude's business case

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Sofia Hill and Jude Murphy. Sofia challenged on ROI and asked tough questions about differentiation from Gong. Presented hard metrics on CS efficiency gains from similar customers. Sofia is supportive but price-sensitive, asked for best and final pricing. Mentioned budget is tight this quarter but sees the value.

questions_asked.question: Why should I pay 30% more than Gong when they also do automation?
questions_asked.answer: Gong's core strength is sales conversation intelligence. Our platform is purpose-built for CS workflows with deeper automation, better CS analytics, and native onboarding tools. Different value propositions.

questions_asked.question: What's your best price for 28 seats if we close this month?
questions_asked.answer: Let me work with my VP on an aggressive first-year rate. Can I get back to you by Friday with our best offer?

objections: Price premium over Gong needs stronger justification
next_steps: Get approval for discounted pricing
Send revised proposal by Friday
Schedule follow-up with Sofia

touches.ai_summary: Second exec meeting with Sofia and Jude. Presented revised proposal at $20,600 ACV (31% discount) for first year, standard pricing on renewal. Sofia appreciates the movement and sees the value but wants to review with finance before committing. Asked about contract terms, payment schedule, and implementation timeline. Riley from RevOps joined to discuss procurement process.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We typically require annual payment, but can discuss quarterly terms with a small adjustment to the rate. Let me check with finance.

questions_asked.question: What's included in ongoing support and are there any additional fees we should know about?
questions_asked.answer: All support, updates, and standard integrations included. No hidden fees. Only additional costs would be if you wanted custom development work, which is optional.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline to make a final decision?
questions_asked.answer: Need to finalize this week to start implementation in May. Riley will drive the contract review and procurement process.

next_steps: Confirm payment terms options
Send final contract to Riley for procurement review
Target close by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Jude and Riley confirming revised pricing at $20,600 ACV with annual payment, or $21,200 with quarterly terms. Sent Master Service Agreement and Order Form for legal review. Riley acknowledged and is routing through procurement.
next_steps: Riley to complete procurement review
Legal review by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Check-in email with Riley on contract review status. Riley responded that legal has a few redlines on data processing terms and liability caps. Sending marked-up version shortly.
next_steps: Review legal redlines
Address data processing and liability terms

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Riley with responses to legal redlines. Accepted most data processing changes, provided standard liability cap language, and flagged two terms that need legal review on our side. Riley confirmed she'll review with their counsel.
next_steps: Riley to review with legal
Schedule negotiation call to finalize terms

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Riley and Jude to finalize contract terms. Agreed on data processing language and liability caps. Riley asked about early termination terms and renewal pricing. Confirmed standard pricing would apply at renewal. Riley comfortable with terms and will route for final signatures.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to add seats mid-contract?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime at pro-rated pricing for the remainder of the term. Seats get folded into standard renewal pricing next year.

next_steps: Riley to route contract for signatures
Target signatures by end of week for May 1 start

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled negotiation call to finalize signatures was rescheduled by Jude. She mentioned Sofia wants to review the renewal pricing terms one more time before signing. New call scheduled for two days out.
next_steps: Rescheduled call to review renewal pricing with Sofia and Jude
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call with Jude, Sofia, and Riley. Sofia confirmed comfort with Year 1 pricing at $20,600 and understands standard pricing applies at renewal. Discussed implementation kickoff timeline and success metrics. Sofia authorized Riley to execute contract. All parties aligned on May 1 implementation start.

questions_asked.question: Who will be our main point of contact during implementation?
questions_asked.answer: You'll have a dedicated implementation manager assigned within 48 hours of contract signature. They'll own the entire onboarding process.

next_steps: Riley to execute contract
Implementation kickoff scheduled for May 3
Assign customer success manager

outcome.churn_reason: did not see enough ROI for the price
